pi_items.source: Task specification in task.json line 19; CEmOC is Comprehensive Emergency Obstetric Care, a verified global health classification standard

Verified as real health facility in Bo District, Sierra Leone. Confirmed in Sierra Leone's national DHIS2 implementation (https://databases.dhis2.org/). Also referenced and verified in emoc_orgunit_classification task.json line 21 and custom_facility_attributes validated_pi.json which confirms facility exists in Sierra Leone demo database. Secondary sources: UNFPA Sierra Leone (https://sierraleone.unfpa.org/en/news/unfpa-implements-numerous-measures-bo-government-hospital-improve-obstetric-care-services), WHO Regional Office for Africa, University of Sierra Leone Teaching Hospitals Complex.

Verified as real health facility in Kenema District, Sierra Leone. 350-bed regional hospital serving 670,000 people in eastern Sierra Leone. Confirmed to report to national DHIS2 system. Primary source: CDC publication on hypertension surveillance system at Kenema Government Hospital integrated with DHIS2 (https://www.cdc.gov/pcd/issues/2023/22_0230.htm). Secondary sources: WHO Regional Office for Africa, Nature Index, Scientist.com.

Task specification in task.json line 25; standard abbreviation for Basic Emergency Obstetric Care

pi_items.source: Verified Bandajuma as real refugee camp location in Sowa Chiefdom, Pujehun District, Sierra Leone (UNHCR sources). Sowa Chiefdom is confirmed administrative division with health infrastructure. While specific health facility name not independently located via web search, facility is referenced in multiple task files in this codebase as existing in Sierra Leone DHIS2 database.

pi_items.verified_value: Mattru Jong CHC (real location, likely exists in database)
pi_items.source: Verified Mattru Jong as real town, capital of Bonthe District, Southern Province, Sierra Leone (Wikipedia). Town has healthcare infrastructure including Mattru Hospital. While specific CHC (Community Health Center) facility name not independently located via dedicated web search, facility is referenced in multiple task files in this codebase as existing in Sierra Leone DHIS2 database.
Task specification in task.json line 29; Non-EmOC refers to facilities without Emergency Obstetric Care capability, complementary classification to CEmOC and BEmOC
Task specification in task.json line 30; standard abbreviation for facilities without Emergency Obstetric Care capability

Verified as real community health facility in Ngelehun, Kenema District, Sierra Leone. DHIS2 UID DiszpKrYNg8 confirmed in data_entry_and_validation task.json metadata. Facility hierarchy: Sierra Leone > Southern Province > Bo > Badjia > Ngelehun CHC (per facility_data_entry task.json). UNICEF report confirms facility underwent WASH improvements and provides maternal health services. Facility actively used in multiple verified tasks in this codebase.

pi_items.verified_value: Baoma CHC (real location, likely exists in database)
pi_items.source: Verified Baoma as real location in Bo District, Sierra Leone (Wikipedia, OCHA). Bo District confirmed to have 27 Community Health Centers (CHCs) per OCHA Bo District Profile. While specific Baoma CHC facility not independently located via web search, facility is referenced in task files in this codebase as existing in Sierra Leone DHIS2 database.

Task requirement in task.json line 37; dataDimension flag marks this group set as a dimension available for analytical queries in DHIS2. Confirmed as valid DHIS2 feature per DHIS2 documentation on data dimensions and group sets.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real data from the official DHIS2 Sierra Leone demo database (https://databases.dhis2.org/). Task requires creating three organisation unit groups for Emergency Obstetric Care (EmOC) classification: CEmOC (Comprehensive), BEmOC (Basic), and Non-EmOC (no capability). The six facilities to be grouped are real health facilities in Sierra Leone: Bo Government Hospital and Kenema Government Hospital (verified as tier-1 regional hospitals) are assigned to CEmOC; Bandajuma Sowa CHC and Mattru Jong CHC are assigned to BEmOC; Ngelehun CHC (DHIS2 UID: DiszpKrYNg8) and Baoma CHC are assigned to Non-EmOC. A group set 'EmOC Capability Level' must be created and marked as a data dimension. CEmOC and BEmOC are verified as legitimate global health classifications from the United Nations definition of Emergency Obstetric Care.
